name: Cover Seeking
description: Cover Seeking is a non-player behavior mechanic that controls sensing, intention, movement, and response.
design_notes: Cover Seeking should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For ai mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use cover seeking as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect cover seeking to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels the world responds with believable intent instead of static scripting.

parameters.name: sense_radius
parameters.description: Area within which the AI can consider stimuli.

parameters.name: decision_interval
parameters.description: How often the AI re-evaluates the behavior.

parameters.name: priority_weight
parameters.description: Relative importance compared with competing AI goals.

edge_cases: Cover Seeking is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Cover Seeking overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Cover Seeking runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Cover Seeking state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Cover Seeking plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Cover Seeking produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Cover Seeking strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Cover Seeking becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Cover Seeking with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Cover Seeking as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Cover Seeking with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Cover Seeking as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
